req.title: Beneficiary management (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-086 defines four testable behaviours: (1) a valid add-beneficiary request returns 201 with status=pending, (2) a duplicate account number returns 409, (3) a repeated request carrying the same Idempotency-Key returns the original 201 response without creating a second record, and (4) the cooling-off state transition is captured. We derive: one EP positive case covering the happy path, one NEG negative case for the 409 duplicate-account path, one BVA boundary case covering exact key re-submission, and one STC negative case verifying the pending state cannot be bypassed within 24 h. The violation fix is: test[3] had kind=negative but heuristic=STC; STC (State Transition Coverage) is compatible with negative kind when the test exercises a forbidden state transition, so the fix is to keep heuristic=STC and correct kind to negative (it was already negative in the original — the real fix is to ensure the heuristic is changed from STC to NEG if STC is reserved for positive state walks, OR keep STC and document it as a negative STC probe). Per the rule that negative kind must not use STC, we change test[3] heuristic from STC to NEG while preserving the state-transition intent of the test.

tests.name: test_add_beneficiary_returns_201_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Account number used does not already exist in customer beneficiary list
Unique Idempotency-Key is generated for this request
actions: POST /beneficiaries with valid account_number, name, and a fresh Idempotency-Key header
expected: Response body contains a non-empty beneficiary_id
cooling_off_expires_at is approximately 24 h from now
trace: R-FIN-086: beneficiary saved in pending state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_add_beneficiary_returns_201_pending(auth_client, unique_account_number):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "name": "Alice Testuser"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = auth_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("beneficiary_id"), "beneficiary_id must be non-empty"

    expires = datetime.fromisoformat(body["cooling_off_expires_at"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    delta = expires - now
    assert timedelta(hours=23, minutes=55) <= delta <= timedelta(hours=24, minutes=5), (
        f"cooling_off_expires_at out of expected 24h window: {delta}"
    )

tests.name: test_add_duplicate_account_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A beneficiary with the target account_number already exists for this customer
A fresh (different) Idempotency-Key is used so idempotency cache is not hit
actions: POST /beneficiaries with an account_number that is already registered for this customer, using a new Idempotency-Key
expected: Response body contains error code DUPLICATE_BENEFICIARY or equivalent
No new beneficiary record is created

tests.name: test_idempotent_resubmission_returns_original_201
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Account number used does not already exist in customer beneficiary list
A specific Idempotency-Key is chosen and stored
actions: POST /beneficiaries with valid payload and Idempotency-Key K (first submission)
Record the full response body and status of the first call
POST /beneficiaries with identical payload and the same Idempotency-Key K (second submission)
expected: Second call returns HTTP 201 (same as first, not 409)
Second response body beneficiary_id is identical to first response body beneficiary_id
Only one beneficiary record exists for this account_number
trace: R-FIN-086: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_idempotent_resubmission_returns_original_201(auth_client, unique_account_number):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"account_number": unique_account_number, "name": "Carol Idempotent"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = auth_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"First call failed: {first.text}"
    first_body = first.json()

    second = auth_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 201, (
        f"Idempotent second call must return 201, got {second.status_code}"
    )
    second_body = second.json()

    assert first_body["beneficiary_id"] == second_body["beneficiary_id"], (
        "beneficiary_id must be identical on idempotent replay"
    )
    assert second_body["status"] == "pending"

    list_resp = auth_client.get("/beneficiaries")
    assert list_resp.status_code == 200
    matching = [b for b in list_resp.json()["beneficiaries"]
                if b["account_number"] == unique_account_number]
    assert len(matching) == 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 record, found {len(matching)}"
    )

tests.name: test_beneficiary_cannot_receive_transfer_within_cooling_off
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A beneficiary was just added and is in pending/cooling-off state
The 24-hour cooling-off window has NOT elapsed
actions: POST /beneficiaries to create a new beneficiary (state: pending)
Immediately attempt POST /transfers targeting that beneficiary_id
expected: POST /transfers returns HTTP 422 or 403 with error indicating cooling-off period active
Beneficiary state remains pending after the rejected transfer attempt
trace: R-FIN-086: 24-hour cooling-off period before beneficiary can receive transfers
R-FIN-086: beneficiary saved in pending state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_beneficiary_cannot_receive_transfer_within_cooling_off(
    auth_client, unique_account_number, transfer_payload_factory
):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    add_resp = auth_client.post(
        "/beneficiaries",
        json={"account_number": unique_account_number, "name": "Dave CoolingOff"},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    )
    assert add_resp.status_code == 201
    beneficiary_id = add_resp.json()["beneficiary_id"]
    assert add_resp.json()["status"] == "pending"

    transfer_payload = transfer_payload_factory(
        beneficiary_id=beneficiary_id, amount="10.00", currency="GBP"
    )
    transfer_resp = auth_client.post("/transfers", json=transfer_payload)

    assert transfer_resp.status_code in (403, 422), (
        f"Transfer to cooling-off beneficiary must be blocked, got {transfer_resp.status_code}"
    )
    err_body = transfer_resp.json()
    err_text = str(err_body).upper()
    assert any(k in err_text for k in ("COOLING", "PENDING", "NOT_ACTIVE", "COOLING_OFF")), (
        f"Error body must reference cooling-off restriction, got: {err_body}"
    )

    beneficiary_resp = auth_client.get(f"/beneficiaries/{beneficiary_id}")
    assert beneficiary_resp.status_code == 200
    assert beneficiary_resp.json()["status"] == "pending", (
        "Beneficiary status must remain pending after rejected transfer"
    )
